title: EDDM Eval Catalog
description: Local review catalog for EDDM mailer proof packages, proof-only concept sets, planning references, scores, and decisions.

candidates.name: V2 six-concept proof set overview
candidates.summary: Side-by-side overview of the six v2 proof-only concepts. Start here to narrow the field before scoring individual concepts.
reviewFocus: Use the contact sheet to pick the two or three concepts worth detailed review.
The README says this pass has no PDFs; pick favorites before a print-ready PDF pass.
Print-safety pick from the manifest: 06_print-safe-classic.
knownLimitations: Proof-only round; no print-ready PDFs in this v2 folder yet.

candidates.name: V2 01: Editorial Rule
candidates.summary: Newspaper-style hairline grid; large headline; restrained green only on the $788K figure.
reviewFocus: Print safety: medium.
Score this individual concept after using the contact sheet for first-pass comparison.
If shortlisted, generate a print-ready PDF in the next pass.
knownLimitations: Proof-only HTML/PNG pair; no per-concept print-ready PDF yet.

candidates.name: V2 02: Structured Columns
candidates.summary: Three-column Swiss-style grid with receipt-style numbers panel and a chart in its own column. Eliminated by David on 2026-05-28 after selecting V2 01 Editorial Rule as the base for revised hybrid exploration.
reviewFocus: Eliminated by David on 2026-05-28 after selecting V2 01 Editorial Rule as the base for revised hybrid exploration.
Print safety: medium-high.
Score this individual concept after using the contact sheet for first-pass comparison.
If shortlisted, generate a print-ready PDF in the next pass.
knownLimitations: Proof-only HTML/PNG pair; no per-concept print-ready PDF yet.

candidates.name: V2 03: Callout Band
candidates.summary: Single dark-ink band carries the headline on the front; white everywhere else. A unifying ink strap on the back.
reviewFocus: Print safety: medium.
Score this individual concept after using the contact sheet for first-pass comparison.
If shortlisted, generate a print-ready PDF in the next pass.
knownLimitations: Proof-only HTML/PNG pair; no per-concept print-ready PDF yet.

candidates.name: V2 04: Paper & Ink
candidates.summary: Warm paper-color field (#FAF8F2 — NOT mint); dark ink type; brand green on the dollar figure and a single keyline.
reviewFocus: Print safety: medium.
Score this individual concept after using the contact sheet for first-pass comparison.
If shortlisted, generate a print-ready PDF in the next pass.
knownLimitations: Proof-only HTML/PNG pair; no per-concept print-ready PDF yet.

candidates.name: V2 05: Chart-Led Front
candidates.summary: Front dominated by the savings curve at scale; headline floats in a small card; back is the calm sibling. Eliminated by David on 2026-05-28 after selecting V2 01 Editorial Rule as the base for revised hybrid exploration.
reviewFocus: Eliminated by David on 2026-05-28 after selecting V2 01 Editorial Rule as the base for revised hybrid exploration.
Print safety: lower-medium.
Score this individual concept after using the contact sheet for first-pass comparison.
If shortlisted, generate a print-ready PDF in the next pass.
knownLimitations: Proof-only HTML/PNG pair; no per-concept print-ready PDF yet.

candidates.name: V2 06: Print-Safe Classic
candidates.summary: Maximum print-friendliness: big type, generous white, solid colors only, thick lines, no hairlines or gradients. Eliminated by David on 2026-05-28 after selecting V2 01 Editorial Rule as the base for revised hybrid exploration.
reviewFocus: Eliminated by David on 2026-05-28 after selecting V2 01 Editorial Rule as the base for revised hybrid exploration.
Print safety: high.
Score this individual concept after using the contact sheet for first-pass comparison.
If shortlisted, generate a print-ready PDF in the next pass.
knownLimitations: Proof-only HTML/PNG pair; no per-concept print-ready PDF yet.

candidates.name: Paired concepts overview
candidates.summary: Six paired Save/Upgrade/Improve concepts with a contact sheet and combined PDF. Use as the second comparison lane against the v2 proof set. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
Use the contact sheet and 6-concept PDF for fast side-by-side review.
Then score individual paired concepts below.
Prioritize high print-safety entries if the printer path is uncertain.

candidates.name: 1. Ledger Rules
candidates.summary: A clean ledger-style grid makes the calculator feel factual and keeps the QR/domain path unmistakable. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
The front uses a structured data-report rhythm, and the back continues it with rule-led columns and a disciplined identity rail.
Print safety: high.
Compare against the matching v2 proof-only concept before naming a finalist.

candidates.name: 2. Editorial Column
candidates.summary: A magazine-like hierarchy gives the $788,000 number more authority without adding decoration or stock imagery. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
The front opens like a feature spread; the back resolves into a left identity column and a strong right-side action lane.
Print safety: medium.
Compare against the matching v2 proof-only concept before naming a finalist.

candidates.name: 3. Statement Border
candidates.summary: A print-friendly black-and-green border creates polish while using almost no tinted area. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
Both sides share the same framed-system language, making this the most conservative printer-friendly direction.
Print safety: high.
Compare against the matching v2 proof-only concept before naming a finalist.

candidates.name: 4. White Space
candidates.summary: More blank space makes the mailer feel premium and lets the domain and QR breathe. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
The front gives the headline generous room; the back mirrors that restraint with sparse benefit blocks and a quiet QR zone.
Print safety: high.
Compare against the matching v2 proof-only concept before naming a finalist.

candidates.name: 5. Proof Strip
candidates.summary: A narrow green proof strip creates brand continuity without becoming a mint background. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
The front uses the strip to anchor assumptions and the back uses it as a visual handoff from advisor identity to action.
Print safety: medium.
Compare against the matching v2 proof-only concept before naming a finalist.

candidates.name: 6. Classic Direct
candidates.summary: A straightforward direct-mail layout maximizes readability for average EDDM printing and quick scanning. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
The front is a simple calculator proof; the back is a direct response layout with clear identity, benefits, QR, and domain.
Print safety: high.
Compare against the matching v2 proof-only concept before naming a finalist.

candidates.name: Back variant 01 Soft Full Wash
candidates.summary: Full light-green wash across the back side; highest visual continuity with the front but likely the least conservative for print. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
Evaluate this as a back-side treatment for the Save / Upgrade / Improve v1 front, not as a standalone full mailer.
Score QR clarity, mailing-area readability, ink load, and whether the back side reinforces the front-side promise.
If selected, regenerate or confirm a complete front/back print-ready PDF with printer-safe margins and USPS/EDDM placement.
knownLimitations: Back-side-only supporting variant; no standalone print-ready PDF for this exact pairing yet.
The shared front proof is included only for context.

assets.label: Shared Save / Upgrade / Improve front proof

candidates.name: Back variant 02 Strong Left Fade
candidates.summary: Green concentration on the left side fading into the QR/action area; stronger brand presence with more white space near the scan zone. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.

candidates.name: Save, Upgrade, Improve EDDM proof set
candidates.summary: Two-sided print-ready proof package built from the Save, Upgrade, Improve structure, with extra back-side variation sheets. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
Review whether the front-side chart and founder proof create a strong enough QR handoff.
Check that the back-side hierarchy makes Save, Upgrade, and Improve readable at mailbox distance.
Compare against the newer v2 and paired-concept sets before selecting a finalist.
knownLimitations: Back-side EDDM indicia/mailing area is a proof layout and must be confirmed with the printer/USPS.
Some variation sheets are exploratory, not final print files.

candidates.name: Restrained EDDM proof set
candidates.summary: Restrained Swiss-style proof set with front/back proofs, contact sheet, production notes, and print-ready PDF. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
Compare as the most conservative design direction against Gradient Stack and Save/Upgrade/Improve.
Check whether restrained hierarchy still has enough mailbox pull.
Use as a print-safe fallback if louder versions feel too busy.

candidates.name: Gradient Stack EDDM proof set
candidates.summary: Earlier gradient-backed proof package with full proof assets and print-ready PDF. Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
reviewFocus: Rejected by David on 2026-05-29; narrowed out in favor of FINALISTS 01 Corrected Editorial Rule.
Use as a contrast reference for why the later v2 pass avoided dominant mint/gradient fields.
Score visual pull separately from print readiness.
Compare back-side readability against the newer no-mint concepts.
knownLimitations: Production notes call out a dominant light-green gradient on the back; that may be less desirable for final print.

candidates.variant: Likely Variant A - Fee Shock / Loss Aversion
candidates.name: 1% Blues corrected-brand EDDM proof set
candidates.summary: Original corrected-brand proof set. Useful baseline for the fee-shock direction and historical comparison.
reviewFocus: Treat as the baseline fee-shock concept.
Compare its mailbox pull against the newer Save/Upgrade/Improve concepts.
Review the corrected brand overlay carefully because the source native design file is not present.
knownLimitations: Corrected PDF/PNGs are raster proof exports with the correct brand overlaid into source artwork.
Printer-ready final should ideally be rebuilt from a native design file if one exists.

candidates.name: Near-identical default-inputs source EDDM pack
candidates.summary: Original extracted EDDM package retained for source comparison. It still contains the source logo treatment and should not be the final brand proof.
reviewFocus: Use for print dimensions, QR/default assumptions, and before/after comparison.
Do not select for production without brand correction and printer confirmation.

candidates.name: RIA Chief EDDM Wave 1 planning bundle
candidates.summary: External local ZIP with the likely earlier A/B/C EDDM planning variants. It has copy and campaign structure, but no front/back/contact-sheet print proofs.
candidates.notPreviewableReason: Files are outside the repo root and are intentionally not served by the eval server until extracted or imported into this repo.
reviewFocus: Use as planning-copy source for Variant A Fee Shock / Loss Aversion, Variant B Fiduciary Expertise / CFA + CFP, and Variant C Keep Control / No Disruption.
Compare the new proof sets against these original strategic hooks before selecting finalists.

candidates.name: QR landing and prototype reference set
candidates.summary: Related landing-page concepts and prototypes. These are not complete EDDM print packages, but they can inform QR-message fit and continuation from the mailer.
reviewFocus: Use only for QR/landing-page fit and creative continuity.
Do not score as print-ready EDDM packages.
